I think part of the reason for why it's specifically Pollux has to do with the different ways the brothers see the MC (as revealed through their quotes and such). To Castor, they're simply his "benefactor", someone who's helping him out, but Pollux has basically made them his new religion - ||given that they're a fragment of the god he was raised to worship, perhaps that magical heart of his recognised something even outside of his conscious awareness.|| I assume the "originally being main story content" thing probably helped as well, of course, but him canonically having this kind of intense view of them is probably part of it
